forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -0,0 +1,34 @@
+---
+assignees:
+- claude-code
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: '8180'
+project: local-review
+title: 'Design: review fleet architecture, dimension taxonomy & finding model'
+---
+## What
+Keystone design task. Produce a written design (`ideas/local-review.md`) that pins the architecture every downstream task builds against. No production code; the artifact is the design doc + the locked decisions below.
+
+Locked decisions (from planning):
+- **Substrate**: a new on-demand `review` MCP tool in `crates/swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/tools/review/`. It reuses the AVP rule loader (rules-as-data) and the ACP agent runner, driven against a git-diff scope — NOT per-tool-call hooks.
+- **Backend**: reuse the existing `ConnectionTo<Agent>` abstraction (see `crates/avp-common/src/validator/runner.rs`). Default to the session agent; allow a config flag to force the local Llama agent for fully-offline review.
+- **Hooks**: the AVP hook execution is retired entirely (separate task). All rules, including safety, run only via on-demand review.
+
+Decide and document in the doc:
+1. **Dimension taxonomy** — the fixed set of review dimensions, each = one fleet agent with a single narrow mandate. Starting set to refine: `duplication-reuse`, `dead-code`, `data-driven-design`, `correctness`, `tests`, `security`, `naming-clarity`, `performance`, plus per-language dimensions. Map the current `review` SKILL.md 7 layers + the existing `builtin/validators/*/rules/*.md` onto these dimensions. The whole point: duplication/reuse/hardcoding each get a DEDICATED agent instead of being layer-2 footnotes a single tired agent rushes.
+2. **Finding data model** — the structured JSON contract a fleet agent returns and the verifier consumes: at minimum `{ file, line, dimension, severity (blocker|warning|nit), claim, evidence, suggestion }`. This becomes the Rust type in the next task.
+3. **Tool op surface** — the `review` MCP op signature: inputs (range / branch / task-id scope, backend override, dimension subset), outputs (structured findings). Mirror the git tool's op-dispatch shape (`crates/swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/tools/git/mod.rs`).
+4. **Reuse map** — explicit list of what is reused vs. built: git `get changes`/`get diff`, code_context `find duplicates`/`get blastradius`/`get callgraph`/`search symbol`, the AVP loader, the ACP runner's parallel/AIMD execution. Call out that the fleet is BATCH-mode (one agent per dimension over all its files), replacing the validator runner's one-agent-per-validator-per-tool-call model.
+5. **Fleet + verify flow** — diagram the stages: scope → match rules → fan out → verify (adversarial refute) → synthesize → kanban. Note the verifier defaults to refuted on uncertainty (ultra-review's "confirmed, not possible" property).
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+- [ ] `ideas/local-review.md` exists with the five sections above filled in concretely (named dimensions, the finding struct fields, the op signature, the reuse map, the staged flow).
+- [ ] Every current review layer and every existing `builtin/validators/**/rules/*.md` rule is mapped to exactly one dimension (a table).
+- [ ] The doc names the exact existing files/types each stage will reuse (git tool, code_context, `ValidatorLoader`, `runner.rs` execution).
+- [ ] Downstream task descriptions on this board are updated to reference the doc's decisions where they were previously TBD.
+
+## Tests
+- [ ] No automated tests — design-only task. Verification is review of the doc against the locked decisions and the existing code it references (paths must resolve).
+
+## Workflow
+- Research-and-write task. Read `builtin/skills/review/SKILL.md`, `crates/avp-common/src/validator/{loader,runner,types}.rs`, and the git/code_context tool mods before writing. Do NOT use `/tdd` (no code).
\ No newline at end of file

-assignees:

-position_column: todo
-position_ordinal: '8180'
-project: local-review
-title: 'Design: review fleet architecture, dimension taxonomy & finding model'
----
-## What
-Keystone design task. Produce a written design (`ideas/local-review.md`) that pins the architecture every downstream task builds against. No production code; the artifact is the design doc + the locked decisions below.
-
-Locked decisions (from planning):
-- **Substrate**: a new on-demand `review` MCP tool in `crates/swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/tools/review/`. It reuses the AVP rule loader (rules-as-data) and the ACP agent runner, driven against a git-diff scope — NOT per-tool-call hooks.
-- **Backend**: reuse the existing `ConnectionTo<Agent>` abstraction (see `crates/avp-common/src/validator/runner.rs`). Default to the session agent; allow a config flag to force the local Llama agent for fully-offline review.
-- **Hooks**: the AVP hook execution is retired entirely (separate task). All rules, including safety, run only via on-demand review.
-
-Decide and document in the doc:
-1. **Dimension taxonomy** — the fixed set of review dimensions, each = one fleet agent with a single narrow mandate. Starting set to refine: `duplication-reuse`, `dead-code`, `data-driven-design`, `correctness`, `tests`, `security`, `naming-clarity`, `performance`, plus per-language dimensions. Map the current `review` SKILL.md 7 layers + the existing `builtin/validators/*/rules/*.md` onto these dimensions. The whole point: duplication/reuse/hardcoding each get a DEDICATED agent instead of being layer-2 footnotes a single tired agent rushes.
-2. **Finding data model** — the structured JSON contract a fleet agent returns and the verifier consumes: at minimum `{ file, line, dimension, severity (blocker|warning|nit), claim, evidence, suggestion }`. This becomes the Rust type in the next task.
-3. **Tool op surface** — the `review` MCP op signature: inputs (range / branch / task-id scope, backend override, dimension subset), outputs (structured findings). Mirror the git tool's op-dispatch shape (`crates/swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/tools/git/mod.rs`).
-4. **Reuse map** — explicit list of what is reused vs. built: git `get changes`/`get diff`, code_context `find duplicates`/`get blastradius`/`get callgraph`/`search symbol`, the AVP loader, the ACP runner's parallel/AIMD execution. Call out that the fleet is BATCH-mode (one agent per dimension over all its files), replacing the validator runner's one-agent-per-validator-per-tool-call model.
-5. **Fleet + verify flow** — diagram the stages: scope → match rules → fan out → verify (adversarial refute) → synthesize → kanban. Note the verifier defaults to refuted on uncertainty (ultra-review's "confirmed, not possible" property).
-
-## Acceptance Criteria
-- [ ] `ideas/local-review.md` exists with the five sections above filled in concretely (named dimensions, the finding struct fields, the op signature, the reuse map, the staged flow).
-- [ ] Every current review layer and every existing `builtin/validators/**/rules/*.md` rule is mapped to exactly one dimension (a table).
-- [ ] The doc names the exact existing files/types each stage will reuse (git tool, code_context, `ValidatorLoader`, `runner.rs` execution).
-- [ ] Downstream task descriptions on this board are updated to reference the doc's decisions where they were previously TBD.
-
-## Tests
-- [ ] No automated tests — design-only task. Verification is review of the doc against the locked decisions and the existing code it references (paths must resolve).
-
-## Workflow
-- Research-and-write task. Read `builtin/skills/review/SKILL.md`, `crates/avp-common/src/validator/{loader,runner,types}.rs`, and the git/code_context tool mods before writing. Do NOT use `/tdd` (no code).
\ No newline at end of file

 ## What
 Keystone design task. Produce a written design (`ideas/local-review.md`) that pins the architecture every downstream task builds against. No production code; the artifact is the design doc + the locked decisions below.

-Locked decisions (from planning):
-- **Substrate**: a new on-demand `review` MCP tool in `crates/swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/tools/review/`. It reuses the AVP rule loader (rules-as-data) and the ACP agent runner, driven against a git-diff scope — NOT per-tool-call hooks.
-- **Backend**: reuse the existing `ConnectionTo<Agent>` abstraction (see `crates/avp-common/src/validator/runner.rs`). Default to the session agent; allow a config flag to force the local Llama agent for fully-offline review.
-- **Hooks**: the AVP hook execution is retired entirely (separate task). All rules, including safety, run only via on-demand review.
+Locked decisions (from planning + follow-up directives):
+- **Engine crate**: rename `avp-common` → **`swissarmyhammer-validators`** and make it the SHARED ENGINE for the pluggable review system. It owns: the rules-as-data loader (hook-free), the finding data model, and the fleet/verify/synthesis orchestration, plus the reusable ACP agent-execution primitive (the bare parallel/AIMD executor extracted from today's `runner.rs`, minus hook coupling).
+- **Tool**: a new on-demand `review` MCP tool in `crates/swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/tools/review/` is a THIN wrapper over the engine crate. It scopes a git diff and calls the engine. No orchestration logic in the tool.
+- **Backend**: reuse the existing `ConnectionTo<Agent>` abstraction. Default to the session agent; allow a config flag to force the local Llama agent for fully-offline review.
+- **Retire AVP entirely**: (a) delete the `avp-cli` app (`apps/avp-cli`) — it is the hook-processor binary; (b) remove the AVP hook-execution machinery (PreToolUse/PostToolUse/Stop chain links, hook context, per-tool dispatch) from the engine crate, KEEPING the loader + the bare ACP executor; (c) remove the AVP validator doctor rule (`check_avp_directory` in `crates/mirdan/src/doctor.rs`, and avp-cli's `doctor.rs` which dies with the app). All rules — including safety — run only via on-demand review; the real-time block on dangerous commands/secret commits is intentionally dropped.

 Decide and document in the doc:
 1. **Dimension taxonomy** — the fixed set of review dimensions, each = one fleet agent with a single narrow mandate. Starting set to refine: `duplication-reuse`, `dead-code`, `data-driven-design`, `correctness`, `tests`, `security`, `naming-clarity`, `performance`, plus per-language dimensions. Map the current `review` SKILL.md 7 layers + the existing `builtin/validators/*/rules/*.md` onto these dimensions. The whole point: duplication/reuse/hardcoding each get a DEDICATED agent instead of being layer-2 footnotes a single tired agent rushes.
-2. **Finding data model** — the structured JSON contract a fleet agent returns and the verifier consumes: at minimum `{ file, line, dimension, severity (blocker|warning|nit), claim, evidence, suggestion }`. This becomes the Rust type in the next task.
+2. **Finding data model** — the structured JSON contract a fleet agent returns and the verifier consumes: at minimum `{ file, line, dimension, severity (blocker|warning|nit), claim, evidence, suggestion }`.
 3. **Tool op surface** — the `review` MCP op signature: inputs (range / branch / task-id scope, backend override, dimension subset), outputs (structured findings). Mirror the git tool's op-dispatch shape (`crates/swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/tools/git/mod.rs`).
-4. **Reuse map** — explicit list of what is reused vs. built: git `get changes`/`get diff`, code_context `find duplicates`/`get blastradius`/`get callgraph`/`search symbol`, the AVP loader, the ACP runner's parallel/AIMD execution. Call out that the fleet is BATCH-mode (one agent per dimension over all its files), replacing the validator runner's one-agent-per-validator-per-tool-call model.
-5. **Fleet + verify flow** — diagram the stages: scope → match rules → fan out → verify (adversarial refute) → synthesize → kanban. Note the verifier defaults to refuted on uncertainty (ultra-review's "confirmed, not possible" property).
+4. **Reuse map** — git `get changes`/`get diff`, code_context `find duplicates`/`get blastradius`/`get callgraph`/`search symbol`, the renamed loader, the extracted ACP executor. The fleet is BATCH-mode (one agent per dimension over all its files), replacing the validator runner's one-agent-per-validator-per-tool-call model.
+5. **Fleet + verify flow** — diagram the stages: scope → match rules → fan out → verify (adversarial refute, defaults to refuted on uncertainty) → synthesize → kanban.
+6. **Migration & teardown map** — what moves where: which avp-common modules survive into `swissarmyhammer-validators`, which are deleted with the hooks, where the safety rule markdown lands as review rules, and the full `avp-common` dependent list to update on rename (`agent-client-protocol-extras`, `apps/swissarmyhammer-cli`, root `Cargo.toml`; `apps/avp-cli` is deleted).

 ## Acceptance Criteria
-- [ ] `ideas/local-review.md` exists with the five sections above filled in concretely (named dimensions, the finding struct fields, the op signature, the reuse map, the staged flow).
+- [ ] `ideas/local-review.md` exists with all six sections filled in concretely.
 - [ ] Every current review layer and every existing `builtin/validators/**/rules/*.md` rule is mapped to exactly one dimension (a table).
-- [ ] The doc names the exact existing files/types each stage will reuse (git tool, code_context, `ValidatorLoader`, `runner.rs` execution).
-- [ ] Downstream task descriptions on this board are updated to reference the doc's decisions where they were previously TBD.
+- [ ] The doc names the exact existing files/types each stage reuses and each teardown item deletes (paths resolve).
+- [ ] Downstream board tasks reference the doc's decisions where previously TBD.

 ## Tests
 - [ ] No automated tests — design-only task. Verification is review of the doc against the locked decisions and the existing code it references (paths must resolve).

 ## Workflow
-- Research-and-write task. Read `builtin/skills/review/SKILL.md`, `crates/avp-common/src/validator/{loader,runner,types}.rs`, and the git/code_context tool mods before writing. Do NOT use `/tdd` (no code).
\ No newline at end of file
+- Research-and-write task. Read `builtin/skills/review/SKILL.md`, `crates/avp-common/src/validator/{loader,runner,types}.rs`, the chain/hook links, `apps/avp-cli/`, `crates/mirdan/src/doctor.rs`, and the git/code_context tool mods before writing. Do NOT use `/tdd` (no code).
\ No newline at end of file

forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -11,21 +11,24 @@

 Locked decisions (from planning + follow-up directives):
 - **Engine crate**: rename `avp-common` → **`swissarmyhammer-validators`** and make it the SHARED ENGINE for the pluggable review system. It owns: the rules-as-data loader (hook-free), the finding data model, and the fleet/verify/synthesis orchestration, plus the reusable ACP agent-execution primitive (the bare parallel/AIMD executor extracted from today's `runner.rs`, minus hook coupling).
-- **Tool**: a new on-demand `review` MCP tool in `crates/swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/tools/review/` is a THIN wrapper over the engine crate. It scopes a git diff and calls the engine. No orchestration logic in the tool.
-- **Backend**: reuse the existing `ConnectionTo<Agent>` abstraction. Default to the session agent; allow a config flag to force the local Llama agent for fully-offline review.
-- **Retire AVP entirely**: (a) delete the `avp-cli` app (`apps/avp-cli`) — it is the hook-processor binary; (b) remove the AVP hook-execution machinery (PreToolUse/PostToolUse/Stop chain links, hook context, per-tool dispatch) from the engine crate, KEEPING the loader + the bare ACP executor; (c) remove the AVP validator doctor rule (`check_avp_directory` in `crates/mirdan/src/doctor.rs`, and avp-cli's `doctor.rs` which dies with the app). All rules — including safety — run only via on-demand review; the real-time block on dangerous commands/secret commits is intentionally dropped.
+- **The validator IS the shard.** There is NO separate "dimension" concept. The fleet runs ONE agent per matched validator. Each validator is authored as a single focused concern (duplication, dead-code, data-driven, security, …). Three nouns only: **validator** (pluggable on-disk unit = one agent's job), **rule** (one check inside a validator), **finding** (one confirmed issue, tagged with its source validator).
+- **Validators are data-driven, declaring their own agent behavior** in frontmatter: `name`, `description` (the agent's mandate), `match.files` (globs), `severity`, and **`probes`** — the list of code_context ops the agent MUST run before reporting (e.g. duplication → `[find_duplicates, search_symbol]`, dead-code → `[get_callgraph, get_blastradius]`). No `trigger` field (nothing hooks).
+- **Tool**: a new on-demand operation-based `review` MCP tool in `crates/swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/tools/review/` is a THIN wrapper over the engine. Ops: `run review` (scope = exactly one of `file`/`glob`/`working`/`sha`; plus `validators?[]` subset, `backend?`), `list validators`, `get validator`, `check validators`. No `install` op (installation is initialization). No `list dimensions` (no dimensions).
+- **Backend**: reuse `ConnectionTo<Agent>`. Default the session agent; allow a flag to force local Llama for fully-offline review.
+- **Retire AVP entirely**: delete `apps/avp-cli`; remove the hook-execution machinery (chain links, hook context, turn-diff sidecars) KEEPING the loader + the bare ACP executor; remove the AVP validator doctor rule (`check_avp_directory` in `crates/mirdan/src/doctor.rs`). All rules — including safety — run only via on-demand review; the real-time block on dangerous commands/secret commits is intentionally dropped.
+- **Directories**: validators load from builtin (embedded) → `~/.validators` (user) → `./.validators` (project), precedence in that order. Builtin set is materialized to disk on init.

 Decide and document in the doc:
-1. **Dimension taxonomy** — the fixed set of review dimensions, each = one fleet agent with a single narrow mandate. Starting set to refine: `duplication-reuse`, `dead-code`, `data-driven-design`, `correctness`, `tests`, `security`, `naming-clarity`, `performance`, plus per-language dimensions. Map the current `review` SKILL.md 7 layers + the existing `builtin/validators/*/rules/*.md` onto these dimensions. The whole point: duplication/reuse/hardcoding each get a DEDICATED agent instead of being layer-2 footnotes a single tired agent rushes.
-2. **Finding data model** — the structured JSON contract a fleet agent returns and the verifier consumes: at minimum `{ file, line, dimension, severity (blocker|warning|nit), claim, evidence, suggestion }`.
-3. **Tool op surface** — the `review` MCP op signature: inputs (range / branch / task-id scope, backend override, dimension subset), outputs (structured findings). Mirror the git tool's op-dispatch shape (`crates/swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/tools/git/mod.rs`).
-4. **Reuse map** — git `get changes`/`get diff`, code_context `find duplicates`/`get blastradius`/`get callgraph`/`search symbol`, the renamed loader, the extracted ACP executor. The fleet is BATCH-mode (one agent per dimension over all its files), replacing the validator runner's one-agent-per-validator-per-tool-call model.
-5. **Fleet + verify flow** — diagram the stages: scope → match rules → fan out → verify (adversarial refute, defaults to refuted on uncertainty) → synthesize → kanban.
-6. **Migration & teardown map** — what moves where: which avp-common modules survive into `swissarmyhammer-validators`, which are deleted with the hooks, where the safety rule markdown lands as review rules, and the full `avp-common` dependent list to update on rename (`agent-client-protocol-extras`, `apps/swissarmyhammer-cli`, root `Cargo.toml`; `apps/avp-cli` is deleted).
+1. **Validator catalog** — the focused validators that replace the current `review` SKILL.md 7 layers + the existing `builtin/validators/*/rules/*.md`. A table mapping every current review layer and every existing rule to exactly one validator, each with its `match.files`, `severity`, and `probes`. The point: duplication/reuse/hardcoding each become their own validator with a dedicated agent + mandatory probe, not layer-2 footnotes one tired agent rushes.
+2. **Finding data model** — `{ file, line, validator, severity (blocker|warning|nit), claim, evidence, suggestion }`.
+3. **Tool op surface** — the four ops above with exact arg/return shapes; mirror the git tool's op-dispatch shape (`crates/swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/tools/git/mod.rs`).
+4. **Reuse map** — git `get changes`/`get diff`, code_context probes, the renamed loader, the extracted ACP executor. The fleet is BATCH-mode (one agent per validator over its files), replacing the runner's one-agent-per-validator-per-tool-call model.
+5. **Fleet + verify flow** — stages: scope (file/glob/working/sha → per-validator work-list) → fan out (one agent per validator, runs its probes) → verify (adversarial refute, defaults to refuted on uncertainty) → synthesize → kanban.
+6. **Migration & teardown map** — which avp-common modules survive into `swissarmyhammer-validators`, which die with the hooks, where the safety rule markdown lands as validators, and the full `avp-common` dependent list to update on rename (`agent-client-protocol-extras`, `apps/swissarmyhammer-cli`, root `Cargo.toml`; `apps/avp-cli` is deleted).

 ## Acceptance Criteria
-- [ ] `ideas/local-review.md` exists with all six sections filled in concretely.
-- [ ] Every current review layer and every existing `builtin/validators/**/rules/*.md` rule is mapped to exactly one dimension (a table).
+- [ ] `ideas/local-review.md` exists with all six sections filled in concretely; the validator catalog table maps every current layer + existing rule to one focused validator with its `probes`.
+- [ ] No "dimension" concept anywhere in the doc; the validator is the shard.

reverse_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -11,24 +11,21 @@

 Locked decisions (from planning + follow-up directives):
 - **Engine crate**: rename `avp-common` → **`swissarmyhammer-validators`** and make it the SHARED ENGINE for the pluggable review system. It owns: the rules-as-data loader (hook-free), the finding data model, and the fleet/verify/synthesis orchestration, plus the reusable ACP agent-execution primitive (the bare parallel/AIMD executor extracted from today's `runner.rs`, minus hook coupling).
-- **The validator IS the shard.** There is NO separate "dimension" concept. The fleet runs ONE agent per matched validator. Each validator is authored as a single focused concern (duplication, dead-code, data-driven, security, …). Three nouns only: **validator** (pluggable on-disk unit = one agent's job), **rule** (one check inside a validator), **finding** (one confirmed issue, tagged with its source validator).
-- **Validators are data-driven, declaring their own agent behavior** in frontmatter: `name`, `description` (the agent's mandate), `match.files` (globs), `severity`, and **`probes`** — the list of code_context ops the agent MUST run before reporting (e.g. duplication → `[find_duplicates, search_symbol]`, dead-code → `[get_callgraph, get_blastradius]`). No `trigger` field (nothing hooks).
-- **Tool**: a new on-demand operation-based `review` MCP tool in `crates/swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/tools/review/` is a THIN wrapper over the engine. Ops: `run review` (scope = exactly one of `file`/`glob`/`working`/`sha`; plus `validators?[]` subset, `backend?`), `list validators`, `get validator`, `check validators`. No `install` op (installation is initialization). No `list dimensions` (no dimensions).
-- **Backend**: reuse `ConnectionTo<Agent>`. Default the session agent; allow a flag to force local Llama for fully-offline review.
-- **Retire AVP entirely**: delete `apps/avp-cli`; remove the hook-execution machinery (chain links, hook context, turn-diff sidecars) KEEPING the loader + the bare ACP executor; remove the AVP validator doctor rule (`check_avp_directory` in `crates/mirdan/src/doctor.rs`). All rules — including safety — run only via on-demand review; the real-time block on dangerous commands/secret commits is intentionally dropped.
-- **Directories**: validators load from builtin (embedded) → `~/.validators` (user) → `./.validators` (project), precedence in that order. Builtin set is materialized to disk on init.
+- **Tool**: a new on-demand `review` MCP tool in `crates/swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/tools/review/` is a THIN wrapper over the engine crate. It scopes a git diff and calls the engine. No orchestration logic in the tool.
+- **Backend**: reuse the existing `ConnectionTo<Agent>` abstraction. Default to the session agent; allow a config flag to force the local Llama agent for fully-offline review.
+- **Retire AVP entirely**: (a) delete the `avp-cli` app (`apps/avp-cli`) — it is the hook-processor binary; (b) remove the AVP hook-execution machinery (PreToolUse/PostToolUse/Stop chain links, hook context, per-tool dispatch) from the engine crate, KEEPING the loader + the bare ACP executor; (c) remove the AVP validator doctor rule (`check_avp_directory` in `crates/mirdan/src/doctor.rs`, and avp-cli's `doctor.rs` which dies with the app). All rules — including safety — run only via on-demand review; the real-time block on dangerous commands/secret commits is intentionally dropped.

 Decide and document in the doc:
-1. **Validator catalog** — the focused validators that replace the current `review` SKILL.md 7 layers + the existing `builtin/validators/*/rules/*.md`. A table mapping every current review layer and every existing rule to exactly one validator, each with its `match.files`, `severity`, and `probes`. The point: duplication/reuse/hardcoding each become their own validator with a dedicated agent + mandatory probe, not layer-2 footnotes one tired agent rushes.
-2. **Finding data model** — `{ file, line, validator, severity (blocker|warning|nit), claim, evidence, suggestion }`.
-3. **Tool op surface** — the four ops above with exact arg/return shapes; mirror the git tool's op-dispatch shape (`crates/swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/tools/git/mod.rs`).
-4. **Reuse map** — git `get changes`/`get diff`, code_context probes, the renamed loader, the extracted ACP executor. The fleet is BATCH-mode (one agent per validator over its files), replacing the runner's one-agent-per-validator-per-tool-call model.
-5. **Fleet + verify flow** — stages: scope (file/glob/working/sha → per-validator work-list) → fan out (one agent per validator, runs its probes) → verify (adversarial refute, defaults to refuted on uncertainty) → synthesize → kanban.
-6. **Migration & teardown map** — which avp-common modules survive into `swissarmyhammer-validators`, which die with the hooks, where the safety rule markdown lands as validators, and the full `avp-common` dependent list to update on rename (`agent-client-protocol-extras`, `apps/swissarmyhammer-cli`, root `Cargo.toml`; `apps/avp-cli` is deleted).
+1. **Dimension taxonomy** — the fixed set of review dimensions, each = one fleet agent with a single narrow mandate. Starting set to refine: `duplication-reuse`, `dead-code`, `data-driven-design`, `correctness`, `tests`, `security`, `naming-clarity`, `performance`, plus per-language dimensions. Map the current `review` SKILL.md 7 layers + the existing `builtin/validators/*/rules/*.md` onto these dimensions. The whole point: duplication/reuse/hardcoding each get a DEDICATED agent instead of being layer-2 footnotes a single tired agent rushes.
+2. **Finding data model** — the structured JSON contract a fleet agent returns and the verifier consumes: at minimum `{ file, line, dimension, severity (blocker|warning|nit), claim, evidence, suggestion }`.
+3. **Tool op surface** — the `review` MCP op signature: inputs (range / branch / task-id scope, backend override, dimension subset), outputs (structured findings). Mirror the git tool's op-dispatch shape (`crates/swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/tools/git/mod.rs`).
+4. **Reuse map** — git `get changes`/`get diff`, code_context `find duplicates`/`get blastradius`/`get callgraph`/`search symbol`, the renamed loader, the extracted ACP executor. The fleet is BATCH-mode (one agent per dimension over all its files), replacing the validator runner's one-agent-per-validator-per-tool-call model.
+5. **Fleet + verify flow** — diagram the stages: scope → match rules → fan out → verify (adversarial refute, defaults to refuted on uncertainty) → synthesize → kanban.
+6. **Migration & teardown map** — what moves where: which avp-common modules survive into `swissarmyhammer-validators`, which are deleted with the hooks, where the safety rule markdown lands as review rules, and the full `avp-common` dependent list to update on rename (`agent-client-protocol-extras`, `apps/swissarmyhammer-cli`, root `Cargo.toml`; `apps/avp-cli` is deleted).

 ## Acceptance Criteria
-- [ ] `ideas/local-review.md` exists with all six sections filled in concretely; the validator catalog table maps every current layer + existing rule to one focused validator with its `probes`.
-- [ ] No "dimension" concept anywhere in the doc; the validator is the shard.
+- [ ] `ideas/local-review.md` exists with all six sections filled in concretely.
+- [ ] Every current review layer and every existing `builtin/validators/**/rules/*.md` rule is mapped to exactly one dimension (a table).
 - [ ] The doc names the exact existing files/types each stage reuses and each teardown item deletes (paths resolve).
 - [ ] Downstream board tasks reference the doc's decisions where previously TBD.

No `install` op (installation is initialization). No `list dimensions` (no dimensions).
+- **Engine crate**: rename `avp-common` → **`swissarmyhammer-validators`** and make it the SHARED ENGINE for the pluggable review system. It owns: the rules-as-data loader (hook-free), the finding data model, the probe registry, and the fleet/verify/synthesis orchestration, plus the reusable ACP agent-execution primitive (the bare parallel/AIMD executor extracted from today's `runner.rs`, minus hook coupling).
+- **The validator IS the shard; the file is the grain.** No "dimension" concept. The fleet runs ONE agent per `(validator, file)` pair. Each validator is authored as a single focused concern (duplication, dead-code, data-driven, security, …). Three nouns only: **validator** (pluggable on-disk unit = one agent's job per file), **rule** (one check inside a validator), **finding** (one confirmed issue, tagged with its source validator).
+- **Validators are data-driven, declaring their own agent behavior** in frontmatter: `name`, `description` (the agent's mandate), `match.files` (globs), `severity`, and **`probes`** — names from the engine probe catalog the engine runs and injects as evidence. No `trigger` field (nothing hooks).
+- **Probes** are engine-run structural facts about a changed entity, NOT agent tool-call instructions. Catalog: `callers` (inbound callgraph of an added symbol — fact), `duplicates` (near-identical blocks for a changed block — fact), `similar` (semantic `search code` on an added function body → reuse candidates — candidate evidence). Fact-probes power a deterministic auto-refute guard in verify; `search_symbol`-by-name and `blastradius`-as-a-check are NOT probes (incoherent / context-only).
+- **Tool** (operation-based, verb-noun, thin wrapper over the engine): the verb `review` dispatches on the scope NOUN — `review file` (path or glob), `review working` (uncommitted vs HEAD), `review sha` (commit/range); shared modifiers `validators?[]`, `backend?`. Plus `list validators`, `get validator`, `check validators`. No `install` op (installation is initialization). No `list dimensions`.
 - **Backend**: reuse `ConnectionTo<Agent>`. Default the session agent; allow a flag to force local Llama for fully-offline review.
 - **Retire AVP entirely**: delete `apps/avp-cli`; remove the hook-execution machinery (chain links, hook context, turn-diff sidecars) KEEPING the loader + the bare ACP executor; remove the AVP validator doctor rule (`check_avp_directory` in `crates/mirdan/src/doctor.rs`). All rules — including safety — run only via on-demand review; the real-time block on dangerous commands/secret commits is intentionally dropped.
 - **Directories**: validators load from builtin (embedded) → `~/.validators` (user) → `./.validators` (project), precedence in that order. Builtin set is materialized to disk on init.

 Decide and document in the doc:
-1. **Validator catalog** — the focused validators that replace the current `review` SKILL.md 7 layers + the existing `builtin/validators/*/rules/*.md`. A table mapping every current review layer and every existing rule to exactly one validator, each with its `match.files`, `severity`, and `probes`. The point: duplication/reuse/hardcoding each become their own validator with a dedicated agent + mandatory probe, not layer-2 footnotes one tired agent rushes.
+1. **Validator catalog** — the focused validators that replace the current `review` SKILL.md 7 layers + the existing `builtin/validators/*/rules/*.md`. A table mapping every current review layer and every existing rule to exactly one validator, each with its `match.files`, `severity`, and `probes`.
 2. **Finding data model** — `{ file, line, validator, severity (blocker|warning|nit), claim, evidence, suggestion }`.
-3. **Tool op surface** — the four ops above with exact arg/return shapes; mirror the git tool's op-dispatch shape (`crates/swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/tools/git/mod.rs`).
-4. **Reuse map** — git `get changes`/`get diff`, code_context probes, the renamed loader, the extracted ACP executor. The fleet is BATCH-mode (one agent per validator over its files), replacing the runner's one-agent-per-validator-per-tool-call model.
-5. **Fleet + verify flow** — stages: scope (file/glob/working/sha → per-validator work-list) → fan out (one agent per validator, runs its probes) → verify (adversarial refute, defaults to refuted on uncertainty) → synthesize → kanban.
-6. **Migration & teardown map** — which avp-common modules survive into `swissarmyhammer-validators`, which die with the hooks, where the safety rule markdown lands as validators, and the full `avp-common` dependent list to update on rename (`agent-client-protocol-extras`, `apps/swissarmyhammer-cli`, root `Cargo.toml`; `apps/avp-cli` is deleted).
+3. **Probe catalog** — the fixed set (`callers`/`duplicates`/`similar`), each as subject (entity from the semantic diff) → code_context op → fact/candidate → which validator consumes it, and which are deterministic (guard-able).
+4. **Tool op surface** — `review file`/`review working`/`review sha` + `list/get/check validators`, with exact arg/return shapes; mirror the git tool's op-dispatch shape (`crates/swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/tools/git/mod.rs`).
+5. **Agent context payload** — the exact per-agent prompt: [change purpose] + [validator mandate + rules + output contract] + [one file: path, structured diff, bounded `source_slice` (header + changed entities + window, NOT whole file), probe evidence]. Plus the file-grain + batching policy.
+6. **Fleet + verify flow** — stages: scope (noun → per-validator per-file work-list with probe results) → fan out (one agent per validator×file, batched) → verify (deterministic probe guard, then adversarial refute) → synthesize → kanban.
+7. **Migration & teardown map** — which avp-common modules survive into `swissarmyhammer-validators`, which die with the hooks, where the safety rule markdown lands as validators, and the full `avp-common` dependent list to update on rename (`agent-client-protocol-extras`, `apps/swissarmyhammer-cli`, root `Cargo.toml`; `apps/avp-cli` is deleted).

 ## Acceptance Criteria
-- [ ] `ideas/local-review.md` exists with all six sections filled in concretely; the validator catalog table maps every current layer + existing rule to one focused validator with its `probes`.
-- [ ] No "dimension" concept anywhere in the doc; the validator is the shard.
+- [ ] `ideas/local-review.md` exists with all seven sections filled in concretely; the validator catalog table maps every current layer + existing rule to one focused validator with its `probes`.
+- [ ] No "dimension" concept anywhere; the validator is the shard and the file is the grain.
+- [ ] The probe catalog lists only coherent probes (subject + question + answer derivable from the diff); the agent context payload and the verb-noun op surface are pinned.
 - [ ] The doc names the exact existing files/types each stage reuses and each teardown item deletes (paths resolve).
 - [ ] Downstream board tasks reference the doc's decisions where previously TBD.

-- **Validators are data-driven, declaring their own agent behavior** in frontmatter: `name`, `description` (the agent's mandate), `match.files` (globs), `severity`, and **`probes`** — names from the engine probe catalog the engine runs and injects as evidence. No `trigger` field (nothing hooks).
-- **Probes** are engine-run structural facts about a changed entity, NOT agent tool-call instructions. Catalog: `callers` (inbound callgraph of an added symbol — fact), `duplicates` (near-identical blocks for a changed block — fact), `similar` (semantic `search code` on an added function body → reuse candidates — candidate evidence). Fact-probes power a deterministic auto-refute guard in verify; `search_symbol`-by-name and `blastradius`-as-a-check are NOT probes (incoherent / context-only).
-- **Tool** (operation-based, verb-noun, thin wrapper over the engine): the verb `review` dispatches on the scope NOUN — `review file` (path or glob), `review working` (uncommitted vs HEAD), `review sha` (commit/range); shared modifiers `validators?[]`, `backend?`. Plus `list validators`, `get validator`, `check validators`. No `install` op (installation is initialization). No `list dimensions`.
-- **Backend**: reuse `ConnectionTo<Agent>`. Default the session agent; allow a flag to force local Llama for fully-offline review.
-- **Retire AVP entirely**: delete `apps/avp-cli`; remove the hook-execution machinery (chain links, hook context, turn-diff sidecars) KEEPING the loader + the bare ACP executor; remove the AVP validator doctor rule (`check_avp_directory` in `crates/mirdan/src/doctor.rs`). All rules — including safety — run only via on-demand review; the real-time block on dangerous commands/secret commits is intentionally dropped.
-- **Directories**: validators load from builtin (embedded) → `~/.validators` (user) → `./.validators` (project), precedence in that order. Builtin set is materialized to disk on init.
-
-Decide and document in the doc:
-1. **Validator catalog** — the focused validators that replace the current `review` SKILL.md 7 layers + the existing `builtin/validators/*/rules/*.md`. A table mapping every current review layer and every existing rule to exactly one validator, each with its `match.files`, `severity`, and `probes`.
-2. **Finding data model** — `{ file, line, validator, severity (blocker|warning|nit), claim, evidence, suggestion }`.
-3. **Probe catalog** — the fixed set (`callers`/`duplicates`/`similar`), each as subject (entity from the semantic diff) → code_context op → fact/candidate → which validator consumes it, and which are deterministic (guard-able).
-4. **Tool op surface** — `review file`/`review working`/`review sha` + `list/get/check validators`, with exact arg/return shapes; mirror the git tool's op-dispatch shape (`crates/swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/tools/git/mod.rs`).
-5. **Agent context payload** — the exact per-agent prompt: [change purpose] + [validator mandate + rules + output contract] + [one file: path, structured diff, bounded `source_slice` (header + changed entities + window, NOT whole file), probe evidence]. Plus the file-grain + batching policy.
-6. **Fleet + verify flow** — stages: scope (noun → per-validator per-file work-list with probe results) → fan out (one agent per validator×file, batched) → verify (deterministic probe guard, then adversarial refute) → synthesize → kanban.
-7. **Migration & teardown map** — which avp-common modules survive into `swissarmyhammer-validators`, which die with the hooks, where the safety rule markdown lands as validators, and the full `avp-common` dependent list to update on rename (`agent-client-protocol-extras`, `apps/swissarmyhammer-cli`, root `Cargo.toml`; `apps/avp-cli` is deleted).
-
-## Acceptance Criteria
-- [ ] `ideas/local-review.md` exists with all seven sections filled in concretely; the validator catalog table maps every current layer + existing rule to one focused validator with its `probes`.
-- [ ] No "dimension" concept anywhere; the validator is the shard and the file is the grain.
-- [ ] The probe catalog lists only coherent probes (subject + question + answer derivable from the diff); the agent context payload and the verb-noun op surface are pinned.
-- [ ] The doc names the exact existing files/types each stage reuses and each teardown item deletes (paths resolve).
-- [ ] Downstream board tasks reference the doc's decisions where previously TBD.
-
-## Tests
-- [ ] No automated tests — design-only task. Verification is review of the doc against the locked decisions and the existing code it references (paths must resolve).
-
-## Workflow
-- Research-and-write task. Read `builtin/skills/review/SKILL.md`, `crates/avp-common/src/validator/{loader,runner,types}.rs`, the chain/hook links, `apps/avp-cli/`, `crates/mirdan/src/doctor.rs`, and the git/code_context tool mods before writing. Do NOT use `/tdd` (no code).
\ No newline at end of file

+---
+assignees:
+- claude-code
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: '8180'
+project: local-review
+title: 'Design: review fleet architecture, validator catalog & finding model'
+---
+## What
+Keystone design task. Produce a written design (`ideas/local-review.md`) that pins the architecture every downstream task builds against. No production code; the artifact is the design doc + the locked decisions below.
+
+Locked decisions (from planning + follow-up directives):
+- **Engine crate**: rename `avp-common` → **`swissarmyhammer-validators`** and make it the SHARED ENGINE for the pluggable review system. It owns: the rules-as-data loader (hook-free), the finding data model, the probe registry, and the fleet/verify/synthesis orchestration, plus the reusable ACP agent-execution primitive (the bare parallel/AIMD executor extracted from today's `runner.rs`, minus hook coupling).
+- **The validator IS the shard; the file is the grain.** No "dimension" concept. The fleet runs ONE agent per `(validator, file)` pair. Each validator is authored as a single focused concern (duplication, dead-code, data-driven, security, …). Three nouns only: **validator** (pluggable on-disk unit = one agent's job per file), **rule** (one check inside a validator), **finding** (one confirmed issue, tagged with its source validator).
+- **Validators are data-driven, declaring their own agent behavior** in frontmatter: `name`, `description` (the agent's mandate), `match.files` (globs), `severity`, and **`probes`** — names from the engine probe catalog the engine runs and injects as evidence. No `trigger` field (nothing hooks).
+- **Probes** are engine-run structural facts about a changed entity, NOT agent tool-call instructions. Catalog: `callers` (inbound callgraph of an added symbol — fact), `duplicates` (near-identical blocks for a changed block — fact), `similar` (semantic `search code` on an added function body → reuse candidates — candidate evidence). Fact-probes power a deterministic auto-refute guard in verify; `search_symbol`-by-name and `blastradius`-as-a-check are NOT probes (incoherent / context-only).
+- **Tool** (operation-based, verb-noun, thin wrapper over the engine): the verb `review` dispatches on the scope NOUN — `review file` (path or glob), `review working` (uncommitted vs HEAD), `review sha` (commit/range); shared modifiers `validators?[]`, `backend?`. Plus `list validators`, `get validator`, `check validators`. No `install` op (installation is initialization). No `list dimensions`.
+- **Backend**: reuse `ConnectionTo<Agent>`. Default the session agent; allow a flag to force local Llama for fully-offline review.
+- **Retire AVP entirely**: delete `apps/avp-cli`; remove the hook-execution machinery (chain links, hook context, turn-diff sidecars) KEEPING the loader + the bare ACP executor; remove the AVP validator doctor rule (`check_avp_directory` in `crates/mirdan/src/doctor.rs`). All rules — including safety — run only via on-demand review; the real-time block on dangerous commands/secret commits is intentionally dropped.
+- **Directories**: validators load from builtin (embedded) → `~/.validators` (user) → `./.validators` (project), precedence in that order. Builtin set is materialized to disk on init.
+
+Decide and document in the doc:
+1. **Validator catalog** — the focused validators that replace the current `review` SKILL.md 7 layers + the existing `builtin/validators/*/rules/*.md`. A table mapping every current review layer and every existing rule to exactly one validator, each with its `match.files`, `severity`, and `probes`.
+2. **Finding data model** — `{ file, line, validator, severity (blocker|warning|nit), claim, evidence, suggestion }`.
+3. **Probe catalog** — the fixed set (`callers`/`duplicates`/`similar`), each as subject (entity from the semantic diff) → code_context op → fact/candidate → which validator consumes it, and which are deterministic (guard-able).
+4. **Tool op surface** — `review file`/`review working`/`review sha` + `list/get/check validators`, with exact arg/return shapes; mirror the git tool's op-dispatch shape (`crates/swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/tools/git/mod.rs`).
+5. **Agent context payload** — the exact per-agent prompt: [change purpose] + [validator mandate + rules + output contract] + [one file: path, structured diff, bounded `source_slice` (header + changed entities + window, NOT whole file), probe evidence]. Plus the file-grain + batching policy.
+6. **Fleet + verify flow** — stages: scope (noun → per-validator per-file work-list with probe results) → fan out (one agent per validator×file, batched) → verify (deterministic probe guard, then adversarial refute) → synthesize → kanban.
+7. **Migration & teardown map** — which avp-common modules survive into `swissarmyhammer-validators`, which die with the hooks, where the safety rule markdown lands as validators, and the full `avp-common` dependent list to update on rename (`agent-client-protocol-extras`, `apps/swissarmyhammer-cli`, root `Cargo.toml`; `apps/avp-cli` is deleted).
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+- [ ] `ideas/local-review.md` exists with all seven sections filled in concretely; the validator catalog table maps every current layer + existing rule to one focused validator with its `probes`.
+- [ ] No "dimension" concept anywhere; the validator is the shard and the file is the grain.
+- [ ] The probe catalog lists only coherent probes (subject + question + answer derivable from the diff); the agent context payload and the verb-noun op surface are pinned.
+- [ ] The doc names the exact existing files/types each stage reuses and each teardown item deletes (paths resolve).
